title: AEye Shares Surge On Key Executive Hire: What Investors Need To Know
content:
AEye Inc shares are rallying Friday morning following the appointment of Paul Berton as the company's new Vice President of Operations and Quality. Here’s what investors need to know.

Former Lucid Motors and Apple Executive Joins AEye Leadership

Berton brings a resume from Lucid Motors, where he led manufacturing and engineering for low-voltage ECUs across more than 30 products. His background also includes leadership roles at Apple and Intel.

Don't Miss:

A single bad hire can set a startup back years. Here are the 5 hires founders most often misjudge — and why Still Learning the Market? These 50 Must-Know Terms Can Help You Catch Up Fast

To secure the hire, AEye's Compensation Committee approved an inducement grant of 125,000 restricted stock units (RSUs) under Nasdaq Listing Rule 5635(c)(4). One-third of these units vest in May 2027, with the remainder vesting quarterly over the subsequent two years.

Berton is tasked with scaling global manufacturing and strengthening supply chain execution as AEye moves further into production.

LIDR Earnings Date: Next Major Catalyst Set for May 13

Looking further out, the next major catalyst for the stock arrives with the May 13 earnings report.

EPS Estimate: Loss of 11 cents (Up from Loss of 33 cents YoY) Revenue Estimate: 28 cents million (Up from 6 cents million YoY)

Image: Courtesy of AEye

title: Earnings week ahead: MSFT, AAPL, GOOG, AMZN, META, XOM, CVX, KO, V, SBUX, F, GM, and more
content:
[Magnifying Glass On Top Of Bar Chart Showing Decling Performance]
DNY59/iStock via Getty Images

As earnings season hits its peak, the week ahead stands out as one of the most consequential reporting stretches of the year.

Five members of the Magnificent Seven, Apple (AAPL [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/AAPL]), Microsoft (MSFT [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/MSFT]), Amazon (AMZN [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/AMZN]), Alphabet (GOOGL [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/GOOGL]), and Meta (META [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/META]), headline the calendar, placing a sharp spotlight on AI monetization, cloud demand, digital advertising, and consumer ecosystems.

Beyond big tech, the week spans a remarkably broad cross-section of the global economy.

Energy majors Exxon Mobil (XOM [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/XOM]), Chevron (CVX [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/CVX]), and ConocoPhillips (COP [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/COP]) will reflect commodity dynamics, while industrial bellwethers Caterpillar (CAT [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/CAT]), General Dynamics (GD [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/GD]), and UPS offer a read on infrastructure, defense, and global logistics demand.

Consumer and payments giants like Coca-Cola (KO [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/KO]), Starbucks (SBUX [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/SBUX]), Chipotle (CMG [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/CMG]), Visa (V [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/V]), and Mastercard (MA [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/MA]) will provide cues on spending resilience, and healthcare leaders Eli Lilly (LLY [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/LLY]), Merck (MRK [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/MRK]), AbbVie (ABBV [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/ABBV]), and Amgen (AMGN [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/AMGN]) will highlight innovation pipelines and demand durability.

The calendar further extends into autos, telecom, fintech, and emerging tech, with Ford (F [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/F]), GM, T-Mobile (TMUS [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/TMUS]), Verizon (VZ [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/VZ]), Robinhood (HOOD [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/HOOD]), Roku (ROKU [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/ROKU]), and Enphase Energy (ENPH [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/ENPH]) adding depth across connectivity, retail investing, and clean energy.

Rounding out the week are names spanning digital platforms, materials, and semiconductors, including QUALCOMM (QCOM [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/QCOM]), Spotify (SPOT [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/SPOT]), Booking Holdings (BKNG [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/BKNG]), First Solar (FSLR [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/FSLR]), and Vale (VALE [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/VALE]).

With leadership stocks and cyclical sectors reporting side by side, this week's earnings will deliver one of the most comprehensive snapshots yet of global growth, corporate spending, and market direction, and a critical test of whether elevated expectations around AI, consumption, and macro resilience can hold.

Below is a rundown of major quarterly updates anticipated in the week of April 20 to April 24:

MONDAY, APRIL 27

VERIZON COMMUNICATIONS (VZ [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/VZ])

Verizon Communications (VZ [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/VZ]) is set to report Q1 results before Monday’s open, with analysts expecting modest Y/Y growth in both revenue and earnings.

Wall Street maintains a Buy rating, while Seeking Alpha’s Quant system remains more cautious at Hold [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/VZ/ratings/quant-ratings#source=firstLevelUrl%3Asymbol%7CbiSectionAsset%3ARatingSummary%7CbiName%3AQuant_rating], citing limited growth visibility.

SA VIEWS REMAIN MIXED:

Kenio Fontes recently downgraded the stock to Hold [https://seekingalpha.com/article/4891357-verizon-why-shareholders-should-fear-the-amazon-flywheel-downgrade], pointing to modest near-term momentum (2–3% revenue growth and 4–5% EPS guidance for 2026) amid intense competition from T-Mobile US and AT&T.

On the bullish side, The Alpha Analyst reiterates a Strong Buy [https://seekingalpha.com/article/4891143-verizon-stock-strong-buy-resilient-cash-flow-stable-valuation], viewing VZ as an income-focused play supported by resilient cash flows, ~9x forward earnings valuation, and robust capital return plans. Management’s focus on ~$5B in cost savings, disciplined capex, and steady free cash flow growth underpins dividend sustainability and buybacks.

In contrast, SA Investing Group Leader Stone Fox Capital maintains a Sell [https://seekingalpha.com/article/4891802-verizon-path-back-down-to-40], citing structural growth challenges, high leverage, and limited exposure to next-generation growth areas. Despite its ~6% yield, concerns around flat wireless growth and reliance on legacy assets could pressure long-term valuation.

 	* Consensus EPS Estimates: $1.21
 	* Consensus Revenue Estimates: $34.86B
 	* Earnings Insight: The company has exceeded EPS expectations in all the past 8 quarters and revenue estimates 50% of the time in that span.

ALSO REPORTING: Nucor (NUE [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/NUE]), Ventas (VTR [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/VTR]), Bed Bath & Beyond (BBBY [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/BBBY]), NOV (NOV [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/NOV]), Domino's Pizza (DPZ [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/DPZ]), Public Storage (PSA [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/PSA]), AvalonBay Communities (AVB [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/AVB]), Cadence Design Systems (CDNS [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/CDNS]), Lending Club (LC [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/LC]), Alliance Resource Partners (ARLP [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/ARLP]), Noble Corporation (NE [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/NE]), Easterly Government Properties (DEA [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/DEA]), and more.

TUESDAY, APRIL 28

COCA-COLA (KO [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/KO])

The Coca-Cola Company (KO [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/KO]) is set to report Q1 results before Tuesday’s open, with analysts expecting over 9% Y/Y growth in both revenue and earnings.

The company recently announced a $1B investment [https://seekingalpha.com/news/4571064-coca-cola-unveils-1b-south-africa-investment-plan-through-2030] in South Africa through 2030, alongside local bottling partners, aimed at expanding production, strengthening distribution, and driving product innovation, highlighting its asset-light, franchise-driven model.

Wall Street maintains a Buy rating, while Seeking Alpha’s Quant system is more cautious at Hold [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/KO/ratings/quant-ratings#source=firstLevelUrl%3Asymbol%7CbiSectionAsset%3ARatingSummary%7CbiName%3AQuant_rating], citing valuation concerns.

SA author YR Research reiterates a Buy [https://seekingalpha.com/article/4890277-coca-cola-the-retirement-dream], emphasizing Coca-Cola’s defensive appeal, consistent mid-single-digit organic growth, and strong dividend profile, with expected annual returns of 8–10%.

In contrast, SA Investing Group Leader Dhierin Bechai downgraded the stock to Hold [https://seekingalpha.com/article/4890598-coca-cola-buy-for-the-dividend-hold-for-the-upside-downgrade], noting limited upside after recent outperformance, with a price target implying modest gains despite stable cash flows and resilient fundamentals.

 	* Consensus EPS Estimates: $0.81
 	* Consensus Revenue Estimates: $12.24B
 	* Earnings Insight: The beverage giant has beaten EPS expectations in 8 straight quarters and revenue in 7 of those reports.

ALSO REPORTING: Visa (V [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/V]), Starbucks (SBUX [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/SBUX]), Enterprise Products Partners L.P. (EPD [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/EPD]), General Motors (GM [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/GM]), BP p.l.c. (BP [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/BP]), Omega HealthCare Investors (OHI [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/OHI]), Expand Energy Corporation (EXE [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/EXE]), W. P. Carey Inc. (WPC [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/WPC]), United Parcel Service (UPS [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/UPS]), Ares Capital (ARCC [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/ARCC]), American Tower Corp. (AMT [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/AMT]), Enphase Energy (ENPH [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/ENPH]), Corning (GLW [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/GLW]), Novartis International AG (NVS [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/NVS]), STAG Industrial (STAG [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/STAG]), WM (WM [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/WM]), Spotify Technology S.A. (SPOT [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/SPOT]), ONEOK (OKE [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/OKE]), NXP Semiconductors N.V. (NXPI [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/NXPI]), Kimberly-Clark (KMB [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/KMB]), Booking Holdings (BKNG [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/BKNG]), T-Mobile US (TMUS [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/TMUS]), Seagate Technology (STX [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/STX]), Mondelez International (MDLZ [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/MDLZ]), Welltower (WELL [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/WELL]), JetBlue Airways (JBLU [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/JBLU]), Opko Health (OPK [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/OPK]), Two Harbors Investment (TWO [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/TWO]), Teradyne (TER [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/TER]), SYSCO (SYY [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/SYY]), Bloom Energy (BE [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/BE]), S&P Global (SPGI [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/SPGI]), Robinhood Markets (HOOD [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/HOOD]), and more.

WEDNESDAY, APRIL 29

MICROSOFT CORPORATION (MSFT [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/MSFT]), AMAZON.COM (AMZN [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/AMZN]), META PLATFORMS (META [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/META]), AND ALPHABET (GOOG [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/GOOG])

Wednesday shapes up as a pivotal moment for Big Tech, with four “Magnificent 7” names. Microsoft (MSFT [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/MSFT]), Meta Platforms (META [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/META]), Amazon (AMZN [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/AMZN]), and Alphabet (GOOGL [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/GOOGL]) are all set to report after the market closes, placing the spotlight firmly on AI, cloud, and digital advertising trends.

Heading into earnings, the narrative remains centered [https://seekingalpha.com/news/4576930-the-earnings-tightrope-will-mag-7-bulls-pivot-to-bottom-fishing] on AI-driven growth versus margin pressure. Microsoft’s Azure momentum is key, though rising CapEx continues to divide opinion. Meta is viewed constructively on pullbacks despite mixed reception to recent AI developments, while Alphabet’s steady growth is tempered by heavy investments in AI and adjacent ventures. Amazon, meanwhile, is doubling down on AI and infrastructure through its massive AWS investments, with markets closely watching returns on capital.

Wall Street maintains a Strong Buy across all four names, while Seeking Alpha’s Quant system takes a more measured stance with Hold ratings, reflecting valuation concerns.

However, SA author Gary Alexander rates Amazon a Sell [https://seekingalpha.com/article/4893417-amazon-sell-on-anthropic-deal-spike-valuation-doesnt-add-up], arguing its ~$2.7T valuation is difficult to justify despite recent momentum driven by AWS and AI partnerships.

Similarly, Dmytro Lebid sees Microsoft as a Sell [https://seekingalpha.com/article/4891641-microsoft-don-t-buy-the-dip-a-30-percent-correction-is-still-ahead], citing rising capital intensity, margin pressure, and potential multiple compression as the business shifts toward infrastructure-heavy AI investments.

Here’s a preview of the key information ahead of the reports: [https://static.seekingalpha.com/uploads/2026/4/24/saupload_MAG7.png]

ALSO REPORTING: Google (GOOGL [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/GOOGL]), Ford Motor (F [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/F]), AbbVie (ABBV [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/ABBV]), Qualcomm (QCOM [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/QCOM]), Teladoc Health (TDOC [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/TDOC]), SoFi (SOFI [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/SOFI]), Teva Pharmaceutical Industries (TEVA [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/TEVA]), Etsy (ETSY [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/ETSY]), GlaxoSmithKline (GSK [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/GSK]), Phillips 66 (PSX [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/PSX]), Biogen (BIIB [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/BIIB]), Chipotle Mexican Grill (CMG [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/CMG]), AstraZeneca (AZN [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/AZN]), Regeneron Pharmaceuticals (REGN [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/REGN]), Sundial Growers (SNDL [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/SNDL]), Lemonade (LMND [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/LMND]), General Dynamics (GD [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/GD]), eBay (EBAY [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/EBAY]), and more.

THURSDAY, APRIL 30

APPLE (AAPL [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/AAPL])

California-based Apple (AAPL [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/AAPL]), another core member of the “Magnificent Seven,” is set to report FQ2 results after Thursday’s close, with analysts expecting ~18% Y/Y EPS growth on ~15% revenue expansion.

Recent developments have kept the spotlight on the iPhone maker. A major leadership transition is underway, with Tim Cook set to move into an Executive Chairman role and John Ternus stepping in as CEO, signaling a stronger product and engineering focus.

Meanwhile, iPhone shipments in China rose 20% in Q1, supported by the latest iPhone cycle, pricing actions, and government incentives.

Wall Street remains bullish, with Morgan Stanley viewing the upcoming print as a potential “clearing event,” driven by solid momentum across iPhone, Mac, and Services.

However, Seeking Alpha’s Quant Rating stays at Hold [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/AAPL/ratings/quant-ratings#source=firstLevelUrl%3Asymbol%7CbiSectionAsset%3ARatingSummary%7CbiName%3AQuant_rating], citing valuation concerns.

SA views are notably divided.

Louis Gerard rates the stock a Sell [https://seekingalpha.com/article/4892801-apple-the-ceo-transition-is-a-multiple-compression-catalyst], flagging elevated multiples, AI execution risks, and potential margin pressure, while Given Mahlangu maintains a Strong Buy [https://seekingalpha.com/article/4892654-apple-stock-see-an-alchain-allen-theorem-at-play-upgrade-strong-buy], pointing to Apple’s pricing advantage and resilient demand dynamics.

Bill Maurer adopts a wait-and-watch [https://seekingalpha.com/article/4892392-apple-enters-new-leadership-era] stance, highlighting strong fundamentals but cautioning that premium valuation may limit near-term upside.

 	* Consensus EPS Estimates: $1.95
 	* Consensus Revenue Estimates: $109.69B
 	* Earnings Insight: Apple has beaten EPS and revenue expectations in 8 straight quarters.

ALSO REPORTING: Altria Group (MO [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/MO]), Merck & Co. (MRK [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/MRK]), Bristol Myers Squibb (BMY [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/BMY]), Mastercard (MA [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/MA]), Caterpillar (CAT [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/CAT]), Roku (ROKU [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/ROKU]), Twilio (TWLO [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/TWLO]), Amgen (AMGN [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/AMGN]), ConocoPhillips (COP [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/COP]), Iron Mountain (IRM [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/IRM]), Southern Company (SO [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/SO]), Roblox (RBLX [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/RBLX]), Valero Energy (VLO [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/VLO]), First Solar (FSLR [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/FSLR]), Eli Lilly & Co. (LLY [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/LLY]), Riot Platforms (RIOT [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/RIOT]), Illumina (ILMN [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/ILMN]), SiriusXM Holdings (SIRI [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/SIRI]), Western Digital (WDC [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/WDC]), Carrier Global (CARR [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/CARR]), Agnico-Eagle Mines (AEM [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/AEM]), Rivian Automotive (RIVN [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/RIVN]), American International Group (AIG [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/AIG]), Tanger Factory Outlet Centers (SKT [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/SKT]), Royal Caribbean Cruises (RCL [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/RCL]), Clorox (CLX [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/CLX]), Cardinal Health (CAH [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/CAH]), Atlassian (TEAM [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/TEAM]), T. Rowe Price Group (TROW [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/TROW]), and more.

FRIDAY, MAY 1

EXXON MOBIL (XOM [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/XOM]) AND CHEVRON (CVX [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/CVX])

Chevron Corporation (CVX [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/CVX]) and Exxon Mobil Corporation (XOM [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/XOM]) are all set to report Q1 results before Friday’s open, with analysts expecting a mixed performance amid a volatile energy backdrop.

Recent developments highlight diverging narratives. Exxon has advanced its LNG ambitions, with its Golden Pass project [https://seekingalpha.com/news/4578804-exxon-qatarenergy-ship-first-lng-cargo-from-golden-pass-export-terminal] shipping its first cargo, while also exploring downstream portfolio optimization, including a potential sale of its Hong Kong retail network. However, Wolfe Research recently downgraded the stock, citing valuation concerns after its strong run.

Chevron, meanwhile, has resumed [https://seekingalpha.com/news/4578829-chevron-resumes-full-production-at-wheatstone-lng-after-cyclone-damage-shutdown] full production at its Wheatstone LNG facility following outage-related disruptions, though operational challenges, including a refinery incident in Texas, could weigh on near-term downstream performance.

Sentiment remains broadly constructive. Wall Street maintains Buy ratings on both names, while Seeking Alpha’s Quant system assigns Strong Buy ratings [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/XOM/ratings/quant-ratings#source=firstLevelUrl%3Asymbol%7CbiSectionAsset%3ARatingSummary%7CbiName%3AQuant_rating], supported by elevated oil prices and geopolitical tailwinds.

SA author views reflect this divergence. Asian Value Investor maintains a Strong Buy [https://seekingalpha.com/article/4889690-chevron-stock-growth-drivers-are-in-sync-with-valuation-and-technicals] on Chevron, citing its upstream leverage, strong balance sheet, and resilience to higher oil prices. In contrast, Exxon is rated Hold [https://seekingalpha.com/article/4889477-exxon-mobil-stock-price-drop-priced-in-everything-but-didnt-deeply-discount-it], with solid fundamentals largely priced in and technical indicators signaling caution despite continued operational strength.

 	* Consensus EPS Estimates: XOM: $1.0; CVX: $1.0
 	* Consensus Revenue Estimates: XOM: $85.26B; CVX: $52.70B
 	* Earnings Insight: XOM has topped EPS expectations in 7 of the past 8 quarters, beating revenue estimates in 6 of those reports, whereas CVX has beaten EPS in 6 and revenue estimates in 5 of those reports.

 ALSO REPORTING: Moderna (MRNA [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/MRNA]), Dominion Energy (D [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/D]), Colgate-Palmolive (CL [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/CL]), Brookfield Renewable Partners (BEP [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/BEP]), Federal Realty Investment Trust (FRT [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/FRT]), TC Energy (TRP [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/TRP]), LyondellBasell Industries (LYB [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/LYB]), Magna International (MGA [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/MGA]), Linde plc (LIN [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/LIN]), Estée Lauder Companies (EL [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/EL]), Newell Brands (NWL [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/NWL]), HF Sinclair Corporation (DINO [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/DINO]), Church & Dwight (CHD [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/CHD]), AON Plc (AON [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/AON]), Lazard (LAZ [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/LAZ]), Cinemark (CNK [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/CNK]), AutoNation (AN [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/AN]), Cboe Global Markets (CBOE [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/CBOE]), and more. 

MORE ON RELATED STOCKS:

 	* Microsoft Is A Value Trap, OpenAI Dependence Could Backfire [https://seekingalpha.com/article/4893897-microsoft-is-a-value-trap-openai-dependence-could-backfire]
 	* What Apple's New Leadership Could Mean For Rising Services Division [https://seekingalpha.com/article/4893553-what-apples-new-leadership-could-mean-for-rising-services-division]

 	* China to restrict US funding in local tech firms after Meta's Manus deal: report [https://seekingalpha.com/news/4579243-china-to-restrict-us-funding-in-local-tech-firms-after-metas-manus-deal]

title: Alphabet Gets Street Backing As Cramer Doubles Down On $400 Target
content:
On CNBC's “Mad Money Lightning Round,” Jim Cramer said Alphabet Inc. is going to $400.

Supporting his view, BMO Capital analyst Brian Pitz, on Wednesday, maintained Alphabet with an Outperform rating and raised the price target from $400 to $410.

JPMorgan analyst Doug Anmuth reiterated an Overweight rating on Monday with a $395 price forecast for Alphabet. The firm named the tech giant a "Top Pick" just as the Google Cloud Next event kicked off in Las Vegas.

Don't Miss:

Think Your ‘Safe' Stocks Protect You? You're Ignoring the Real Growth Triggers — Here's What to Add Now Caught With Nothing Saved for Retirement? These 5 Game‑Changing Tips Could Still Save You

Also, PepsiCo entered a multi-year agreement to deploy Google Cloud’s Gemini Enterprise Agent Platform, strengthening its digital infrastructure and advancing its multi-cloud strategy. The partnership aims to address key areas, including supply chain management and go-to-market execution.

Cramer added Arm Holdings plc to the “buy” list of his Charitable Trust. The stock gained 21 points on Wednesday.

“This is the type of thing I'm talking about. You have to be in it," Cramer added.

Lending support to his choice, Susquehanna analyst Chris Rolland, on April 16, maintained ARM Holdings with a Positive rating and raised the price target from $170 to $210.

Photo via Shutterstock
